title: pedicularis rupicola subsp. rupicola
intro: Pedicularis rupicola subsp. rupicola is a subspecies of flowering plant in the balm family (Lamiaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States, commonly known as 'Mountain Lousewort'. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Pedicularis rupicola subsp. rupicola include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
